column1: MERGE MECHANICS REFERENCE
Quick reference for the merge system — use when documenting unit behaviors and board state logic
CORE RULE
Two units of the SAME TYPE and the SAME MERGE RANK can be merged together.
The result is ONE RANDOM UNIT from the player's 5-card deck at RANK +1.
The player does NOT choose which unit comes out of a merge — it is random within the deck.
NOTABLE EXCEPTIONS
Some units, heroes, spells, or artifacts can produce non-standard merge results.
Document any exceptions in the relevant unit / spell / artifact sheet.
BOARD OPENING MECHANICS (other than merging)
• Hero abilities — some heroes can remove or relocate units, opening board slots
• Specific unit abilities — e.g. Portal Keeper can move units between cells
• Spells — some spells may open slots, duplicate units, or manipulate board layout
• Artifacts — some artifacts may have passive or active board effects
• Trainer dummies — occupy slots temporarily, then expire and clear the slot
IMPLICATIONS FOR ANALYSIS
• A unit appearing in a cell is NOT necessarily a newly summoned unit — it may be a merge output or a relocated unit
• The board distribution at any moment is shaped by merge luck as well as deck choice
• Merge rank + wave number must be evaluated together (high rank early = efficient play)
• The vision system must track unit identity across frames by position to detect movement vs. new summons
